brief_summary: Three-panel cross-section comparing the Durock T1's leaf-crossing bump mechanism against the Gazzew Boba U4T's leaf-crossing bump and Topre's capacitive dome-collapse. Panels one and two share a mechanism family (MX-format stem leg crossing a metal leaf); panel three is structurally unrelated (rubber dome over a conical spring read by a capacitive PCB pad). The diagram grounds the article's central claim that the Topre comparison builders draw is about felt character, not shared architecture.
data_sources: data/switches/durock-t1.json — 'Heavy premium tactile with a sharp, pronounced bump near the top of the stroke' and spring 67g/70g
data/switches/gazzew-boba-u4t.json — spring 62g/68g, all-nylon housing, 'pronounced round bump'
durock-t1-deep-dive.mdx § 'The bump: a sharp front edge, minimal plateau' — 'a POM stem with a steeper leading shoulder than the U4T's rounded one'
durock-t1-deep-dive.mdx § 'Why builders reach for the Topre comparison' — 'A Topre keypress is not a leaf crossing at all ... mechanically unrelated to that architecture in every particular'
apps/web/src/content/articles/topre-electrocapacitive-deep-dive.mdx § 'The architecture, precisely' — conical spring, rubber dome, capacitive PCB pad, no contact closure
